scores.head_floor_note: HEAD distance is 149 because the function is held by an asmfix `replace_with_asmfile` bridge with a stub C body; when the bridge is stripped + the cheat-asm injection (371 lines) is stripped, the stub builds to build_insns=2. The 149 measurement is a sandbox-strip artifact, NOT a reflection of how close pure-C could be. The candidate's 52 is the FIRST honest pure-C measurement and is below the prior session's reported 76 floor.
scores.note: Score 52 / build_insns 151 EXACT match (target_insns 151) reproduces in current main when the candidate.c externs are ALL included — critical extern is  since the existing decl in src/display.c at line 837 is AFTER the function deployment site at line 710. Without it, cc1 treats D_8009BF48 as implicit and the function scores 55 / build_insns 129. See notes.md CORRECT DEPLOYMENT RECIPE section.

sessions.lever: session 2: semantic reconstruction — GPU GP0 DMA chain packet builder. 2-arg signature (arg2/arg3 unused), goto-form clamps mirroring sibling func_8007CE0C, big-packet vs small-packet branch based on `(*(u16*)arg0 & 0x3F) || ((u16)arg0->w & 0x3F)`. Levers: m2c-shape 2-arg sig, extern s32 *D_8009BF48 forward decl, address-of D_800F187C captured into local p187C (saves lui/sw pair), *(u16*)arg0 & 0x3F (matches target's lhu instead of lw), goto-based clamps (nested-if scored 87, goto form 67 then 52).

session 3 (this session): 9 additional SOTN-allowed structural variants (variable reuse, named intermediates, operand-order swap, store reordering, signature change) — all confirm sandbox 52 floor with 0 rules retired. Cumulative ~24 negative levers across two sessions.
sessions.note: No further floor reduction; the residual 52 is list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the GPU packet store sequence.

sessions.lever: workflow round 1 (session 4): confirmed sandbox-52 baseline via direct disable=all build and tested 6 additional structural variants from the score-52 base. All confirm the 52 floor.
sessions.note: Candidate.c body is structurally end-to-end correct (151/151 insns, $t0/$t1/$s0/$s1 register match, frame 0x40 match, packet-path store address sequences match target). Residual 52 is pure list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY in the GPU packet store sequence: target splits 0x03FFFFFF constant materialization across ~36 instructions (lui $a3 at idx 49, ori $a3 at idx 85) and interleaves BF48 OT-link load chain with mode-constant stores; my build emits them adjacent. Six new SOTN-allowed C-source restructures (store-order swap, byte-pointer flag check, typed constant, struct field signedness, parenthesization, cast variants) all confirmed the 52 floor. Cumulative ~30 negative levers across 4 sessions. Lever class matches cpu_side_move_dir_4 sched.c walls.

sessions.lever: workflow round 3: deployment probe — applied candidate body to display.c + commented out asmfix bridge to enable measurement, attempted full build
sessions.note: NEW META-BLOCKER FINDING: deploying any pure-C candidate for func_8007CBB0 to a score-0 SHA1-matching state would BREAK sibling func_8007CE0C's 3 splice rules (regfix.txt:4208, 4210, 4212) unless those rules are first migrated to the new `{lbl#N}` function-local label slot mechanism (per global-label-drift-sibling-cheat.md as updated 2026-06-02). This is a META-BLOCKER on the queue item, not just a score gap. Sandbox CBB0 itself was unscorable in this session (same display.c-wide cheat-asm-strip pipeline truncation as C7A0/C97C); the floor-52 measurement remains from prior session-2/session-3/round-1. The chain-extender lever that worked for cpu_side_move_dir_4 is now FORBIDDEN (codified in global-label-drift-sibling-cheat.md FORBIDDEN section 2026-06-02), so the natural negative-label-drift counter-lever is closed. No measurable progress; rejected_forms updated with the deployment-blocker evidence.

sessions.lever: orchestrator deployment verification (post round-3): candidate body applied to src/display.c with asmfix bridge disabled; full build SUCCEEDED (no CE0C splice-rule breakage), refuting round-3 worker claim
sessions.note: EMPIRICAL REFUTATION of round-3 worker meta-blocker claim. The worker said deploying CBB0 candidate breaks CE0C splice rules at regfix.txt:4208/4210/4212 referencing .L152/.L154/.L158/.L174. Orchestrator audit: CE0C splice rules in current main already use {lbl#N} function-local slots (regfix.txt:4184/4186/4188/4189); the cited absolute-label form does not exist in main. Worker was reading a stale worktree regfix.txt. Verification recipe: (1) revert src/display.c stub + asmfix bridge to clean state; (2)  to set build/ to oracle bytes (SHA1 62efab4f...); (3) apply candidate.c body to src/display.c LEAVING bridge active in asmfix.txt; (4)  returns score 55, build_insns 129, target 151, scorable=true. Sandbox builds candidate WITHOUT the bridge (rules_dropped=1), compares against build/ which has the bridge-injected oracle bytes. NEW DISCREPANCY FOUND: prior WIP claimed score 52 / build_insns 151 (exact match). Current measurement is score 55 / build_insns 129 (22 insns short). Candidate or environment has drifted. Worth a focused investigation in round 4 — the structural reconstruction is still valid but the residual is now different than the WIP documents. Source reverted; verify-oracle --rebuild SHA1 == oracle (62efab4f...) confirmed.

sessions.lever: orchestrator re-verification: discrepancy resolved — missing extern s32 *D_8009BF48 declaration before function (existing decl at line 837 was AFTER deployment site at line 710)
sessions.note: Round-2 worker correctly measured score 52 / build_insns 151 EXACT in session 2 (2026-06-01). Round-3 + orchestrator deployment attempts forgot to include the extern s32 *D_8009BF48 declaration before func_8007CBB0 (the candidate.c file has it; the deployment edits to src/display.c omitted it because an extern decl already exists at line 837, but that line is AFTER where the function lands at line 710 so it doesn't cover the function body). Re-deployment WITH the extern added: sandbox --disable all --keep-cheat-asm returns score=52, build_insns=151, target_insns=151. The score-52 floor IS reproducible.

reviewer.evidence: Not yet invoked on the candidate. The candidate is structural pure-C with no obvious cheat constructs (no register pins, no register-asm, no volatile coercion on globals, no unused locals — the (void)arg2/arg3 is the K&R-style ABI honoring of unused params, see SOTN community standard). Invoke cheat-reviewer before any Match: commit. Manual self-audit notes the candidate's `(void)arg2; (void)arg3;` declarations have semantic purpose (silence unused-param warnings on a function whose ABI takes 4 args but logic uses 2 — the calling convention is what matters for the prologue size, and this preserves it cleanly). [2026-06-03 update] Score-52 candidate body verified reproducible (build_insns 151 == target 151). Reviewer still owes an independent PASS verdict on the candidate.c body before any Match: commit attempt.

remaining_gap.summary: List-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY decisions in the GPU packet store sequence. Target interleaves the 0x03FFFFFF constant materialization (lui $a3 0x3FFFFFF + ori $a3 0xFFFF split by ~20 insns) with the BF48 load chain; mine emits them adjacent. Same class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec sched.c walls. NO frame coercion, NO register allocation diffs — pure scheduling.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD's single rule is `func_8007CBB0: replace_with_asmfile "asm/funcs/func_8007CBB0.s"` — an asmfile bridge over a stub body. The candidate's score-52 form is structurally and semantically correct; to commit, the bridge rule would be retired (drop the bridge → replace stub with candidate body) only if score reaches 0. The structural shape is the right base for the close; the residual is scheduling-only.

next_hypotheses: Score-52 floor confirmed. Round 4 should focus on the LIST-SCHEDULER residual — the 0x03FFFFFF lui/ori constant materialization that target splits across ~36 instructions but the candidate emits adjacent. Same INSN_PRIORITY tiebreaker class as cpu_side_move_dir_4 sched.c walls. Try BB2_SCHED_DEBUG instrumented cc1 dump on the candidate to identify exactly which RTL edges create the constant-materialization adjacency.

Directed permuter from CLEAN single-function offset-0 target with the candidate as base. The 52 residual is structural-scheduler, not register-allocation or control-flow, so permuter randomization may find a C structure that nudges sched.c's priority calculation for the constant-materialization pseudo. CRITICAL: vet output against the cheat catalog AND invoke cheat-reviewer agent before accepting any sub-52 candidate. The chain-extender lever family (forbidden 2026-06-02) is the natural attractor in this search space.

Workflow round 3 (2026-06-03) PRIORITIZED next-step menu — round 4 should ATTEMPT the {lbl#N} migration of func_8007CE0C's 3 splice rules (regfix.txt:4208, 4210, 4212) FIRST as a precondition. Recipe: (1) probe cc1's current label sequence for func_8007CE0C via `bash tmp/probe_func_labels.sh func_8007CE0C src/display.c`; (2) identify slot # for each of .L152/.L154/.L156/.L158/.L159/.L160/.L174; (3) rewrite each splice rule replacing literal labels with `{lbl#N}` placeholders; (4) verify-oracle --rebuild — bytes don't change, confirms migration is correct; (5) NOW the candidate body for CBB0 can be deployed and measured without breaking CE0C. ALSO: the sandbox-noscoring gotcha for display.c (sibling index-based reorder rule breaking on cheat-asm strip) is a separate meta-blocker; the C86C round-3 worker's `--keep-cheat-asm` workaround should work for CBB0 too (this function's HEAD body is the stub-with-bridge form; the candidate has no cheat-asm). If both infrastructure migrations land, THEN execute the original directed-permuter hypothesis from a CLEAN single-function offset-0 target. CRITICAL: any saved permuter candidate below 52 must be vetted against the cheat-by-any-spelling catalog AND submitted to the cheat-reviewer agent — the dead-goto label-pad and DImode chain levers are exactly the cpu_side_move_dir_4-style chain extenders FORBIDDEN 2026-06-02.

Run with --stop-on-zero and PERM_GENERAL directed macros targeting scheduling permutations. Vet any saved candidate below 52 via the cheat-reviewer agent against cheats-by-any-spelling policy.

structural_evidence.build_insns_match: 151 candidate == 151 target — EXACT match. Structural decomp is end-to-end correct.
structural_evidence.register_match: arg0 in $t0, arg1 in $t1, $s0 holds 0xE3000000, $s1 holds 0xE5000000 — all match target.
structural_evidence.small_packet_stores: 6 stores in order F1858/F185C/F1864/F1860/F1868/F186C — matches target.
structural_evidence.clamp_shape: Both clamps emit `lh; nop; addu v1,v0,zero; addiu v0,v0,-1; slt; bnez` (matches target shape, including the 'save BE78 into $v1' trick).
